راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.6.0 [ edit ] Buff: modified and increased the number of affected Survivors from a variable 2 / 3 / 4 to 4 Survivors across all Tiers.
Nerf: modified and reduced the Regression percentage on Good Skill Checks from a fixed -5 % across all Tiers to a variable 3 / 4 / 5 %.
Patch 3.5.0 [ edit ] Rework: Hex: Ruin now causes all Generators to regress anytime a Survivor is not repairing them. The regression speed is set to 100 / 150 / 200 %.
Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Nerf: halved the Regression speeds from 100 / 150 / 200 % to 50 / 75 / 100 %.
Nerf: now deactivates after the first Survivor is killed.
Patch 7.5.0 [ edit ] Reversal: no longer deactivates after the first Survivor is killed.
Patch 9.2.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Regression speeds from 50 / 75 / 100 % to 100 / 125 / 150 %.

Calculations [ edit ] Perk Tier Regression speed Regression rate Full Regression time (from ~90 Charges) Tier I 100 % -0.25 c/s ~360 seconds (6 minutes) Tier II 125 % -0.3125 c/s ~288 seconds (4 minutes, 48 seconds) Tier III 150 % -0.375 c/s ~240 seconds (4 minutes) <span class="mw-headline"

name: Hex: The Third Seal
official_description: "A Hex that hinders the Survivors' Aura-reading abilities." The last 2 / 3 / 4 Survivors you hit with a Basic or Special Attack suffer permanently from the Blindness Status Effect. The Hex Effects persist until the Hex Totem is either blessed or cleansed by a Survivor, disabling it for the remainder of the Trial. "She touched your skin, you bear the witch's mark!"

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.4.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: added the ability to also suppress the Aura-reading of Maps.
Patch 1.9.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: updated the Perk with the Blindness Status Effect.
Patch 5.3.0 [ edit ] Buff: the effect is now triggered by both Basic and Special Attacks.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Hex: Thrill of the Hunt
official_description: "A Hex rooting its power on hope. The false hope of Survivors fills you with excitement and strengthens your Totems." Start the Trial with 5 Tokens, one for each Totem located in the environment: Reduces the Action speeds for Blessing and Cleansing by a stack-able 8 / 10 / 12 % per Token, up to a maximum of 40 / 50 / 60 %. | Hex: Thrill of the Hunt loses -1 Token whenever a Totem is cleansed. The Hex Effects persist until the Hex Totem is either blessed or cleansed by a Survivor, disabling it for the remainder of the Trial.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.5.0 [ edit ] Rework: reworked how the Perk obtains its Tokens, no longer requiring that a Survivor must be unhooked from a certain distance away. each standing Totem in the environment grants a Token.
each cleansed Totem removes a Token.
Quality of Life: Survivors starting to cleanse any Hex Totem triggers a Loud Noise Notification.
Patch 1.5.2 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Cleansing Speed penalty from 4 / 7 / 10 % per Token to 4 / 5 / 6 % per Token.
Change: increased the bonus Bloodpoint gains from 4 / 6 / 8 % per Token to 6 / 8 / 10 % per Token.
Patch 2.6.0 [ edit ] Change: modified the bonus Bloodpoint gains from a variable 6 / 8 / 10 % per Token to a fixed +10 % per Token across all Tiers.
Patch 5.3.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Action Speed penalty from 4 / 5 / 6 % per Token to 8 / 9 / 10 % per Token.
Quality of Life: updated the Perk to include the newly introduced Blessing action for Boon Perks.

name: Hex: Two Can Play
official_description: "A Hex that teaches those who mess with you a lesson." Anytime you are stunned or blinded by any Survivor a total of 4 / 3 / 2 times, and if there is not yet a Hex Totem already associated with Hex: Two Can Play and there is at least one Dull Totem remaining in the Trial Grounds, Hex: Two Can Play activates on a random Totem: Blinds all Survivors who stun or blind you for 1.5 seconds. This does not affect carried Survivors. | | The Hex Effects persist until the Hex Totem is either blessed or cleansed by a Survivor, disabling it for the remainder of the Trial. "From now on, no more Mr Good Guy." — Chucky

name: Hex: Undying
official_description: "A Hex which maintains the vile powers that flow throughout the Trial." Whenever a Survivor is within 2 / 3 / 4 metres of a Dull Totem, Hex: Undying triggers its primary effect: Their Aura is revealed to you for as long as they remain in that range. | Whenever a Hex Totem is cleansed, Hex: Undying triggers its secondary effect: Transfers the cleansed Hex to the Hex Totem associated with Hex: Undying instead, replacing it. | The Hex Effects persist until the Hex Totem is either blessed or cleansed by a Survivor, disabling it for the remainder of the Trial.
description_ar: بيرك Hex: Undying يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Blight. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 4.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: now reveals a Survivor's Aura for as long as they are within the activation range.
Buff: changed the activation range to be variable ( 2 / 3 / 4 metres ).
Buff: now transfers Tokens of the Hex perk.
Nerf: now only reveals Survivors near Dull Totems.
Nerf: in case of Survivors cleansing another Hex Totem, Hex: Undying will now only transfer it once, then be deactivated.
Nerf: no longer reveals a Survivor's Aura for 4 / 5 / 6 seconds outside of the Totem's range.

name: Hex: Wretched Fate
official_description: You see your victim from afar and resolve to prolong their suffering. Your effort will be for naught!" — Dracula

name: Hoarder
official_description: You protect what little you have and are perceptive to those rummaging through your stockpiles. Hoarder triggers a Loud Noise Notification for 4 seconds, whenever a Survivor performs any of the following actions within 32 / 48 / 64 metres of your location: Unlocks a Chest. | Picks up any Item. | Hoarder spawns 2 additional Chests in the Trial.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 4.4.1 [ edit ] Buff: increased the distances to 32 / 48 / 64 metres.
Nerf: removed the stipulation that the Rarity of Items found in Chests is decreased. This effect was never actually implemented due to technical issues, and was not functional even before this Patch.
Patch 7.0.0 [ edit ] Nerf: no longer triggers a Loud Noise Notification when a Limited Item is picked up.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 3.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: removed the Basic Attack condition Infectious Fright now triggers whenever a Survivor is put into the Dying State by any means.
Patch 3.4.0 [ edit ] Nerf (undocumented): no longer affects Survivors that are hiding in Lockers.

Patch 6.6.0 [ edit ] Change: modified the behaviour of the triggered Survivor screams to be consistent across all Unlockables: Survivor screams are now 3D with a maximum audible range of 19 metres (used to be a map-wide 2D scream).
Triggers a Loud Noise Notification.

Patch 6.6.2 [ edit ] Reversal: reverted the behaviour of Survivor screams to their original functionality. The reversal was motivated by the community's negative response to the range limit, which caused the Perks to end up weaker than before.

Nerf: reduced the Wiggle-Time increase from 20 / 40 / 60 % to 15 / 27 / 36 %.
Patch 1.5.0 [ edit ] Reversal: reverted the previous nerf to the Involuntary-Strafing reduction from 20 / 40 / 60 % back to 25 / 50 / 75 %.
Nerf: further reduced the Wiggle-Time increase from 15 / 27 / 36 % to 4 / 8 / 12 %.
Patch 2.6.3 [ edit ] Buff: modified and increased the Involuntary-Strafing reduction from a variable 25 / 50 / 75 % to a fixed -75 % across all Tiers.

This effect lingers for 4 seconds. | | Causes them to suffer from the Exposed Status Effect for 30 seconds. | "This is no place for cowards." — The Legion

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.5.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Linger duration from 4 seconds to 3 seconds.
Patch 3.2.0 [ edit ] Undocumented Reversal: reverted the Linger duration from 3 seconds back to 4 seconds.
Patch 4.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: doubled the duration of the Exposed Status Effect from 15 seconds to 30 seconds.

Patch 6.6.0 [ edit ] Change: modified the behaviour of the triggered Survivor screams to be consistent across all Unlockables: This change would have modified the Survivor scream from a map-wide 2D scream to a 3D scream with a maximum audible range of 19 metres.

Patch 6.6.2 [ edit ] Reversal: reverted the behaviour of Survivor screams to their original functionality. The reversal was motivated by the community's negative response to the range limit, which caused the Perks to end up weaker than before.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Keep Them Waiting
official_description: Damaging any Survivor other than the Obsession with a Basic Attack causes Keep Them Waiting to gain +1 Token, up to a maximum of 6 / 7 / 8 Tokens. Damaging the Obsession with either a Basic or Special Attack causes Keep Them Waiting to lose -2 Tokens: Grants a stack-able -4 % per Token Cool-down time reduction on successful Basic Attacks, up to a maximum of 24 / 28 / 32 %. | Keep Them Waiting freezes its current Token Count once the Obsession is sacrificed or killed. You can only be obsessed with one Survivor at a time.
description_ar: بيرك Keep Them Waiting يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح.  أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Cool-down Time reduction for successful attacks from 25 / 29 / 33 % to 30 / 35 / 40 %.
Nerf: added an Activation condition that requires chasing a Survivor for a minimum of 20 seconds to activate the Perk, which lingers for 5 seconds if the chase is lost.

Nerf: no longer affects missed attacks. This nerf was motivated by the excessive use of the so-called "Machine Gun Combo" amongst Killer Players with Unrelenting. Since both Perks had complementary effects, stacking them together reduced the Cool-down Time of Basic Attacks to problematic levels.

Patch 2.1.0 [ edit ] Rework: the Cool-down Time reduction is now based on the amount of accumulated Tokens and there is no longer an Activation condition. This change was motivated by the previous iteration feeling unreliable to use, often requiring artificially prolonging chases to get any benefit from it.
Patch 7.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: changed the Token loss from a variable 4 / 3 / 2 Tokens to a fixed -2 Tokens across all Tiers.

Nerf: Special Attacks on the Obsession now also lose Tokens. This change was motivated by M2 Killers having previously had the unfair advantage (compared to M1 Killers) of circumventing the loss of Tokens by exclusively using their Power to attack the Obsession (e.g. Shred on The Demogorgon ).
Nerf: reduced the Cool-down Time reduction from a stack-able -5 % to -4 %.
Nerf: changed the maximum Token count from a fixed 8 Tokens to a variable 6 / 7 / 8 Tokens.

name: Knock Out
official_description: "The trauma caused by your brutal attacks makes crying for help painfully difficult." Whenever a Survivor moves farther than 6 metres from a Pallet within 6 seconds of dropping it, Knock Out triggers its effect: Causes them to suffer from a -5 % Hindered Status Effect for 3 / 4 / 5 seconds. | "Oh, that gun's no good. The old way... with a sledge! You see, that way's better. They die better that way." — Nubbins Sawyer

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 4.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: now causes downed Survivors to temporarily have no Aura-reading abilities and to crawl at a slower pace.
Nerf: now only triggers on Basic Attacks.
Quality of Life: now temporarily causes downed Survivors to be deafened, while applying a tunnel-vision effect.
Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: now temporarily reduces the Recovery speed of Survivors.

Patch 8.6.0 [ edit ] Rework: reworked the Perk to address its main use as a slugging-tool. Now applies the Hindered Status Effect to Survivors leaving the nearby area around a Pallet they recently dropped.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Languid Touch
official_description: "Your dreadful aura is so suffocating that even terrifying scenes tire your prey." When a Survivor scares off a Crow within 36 metres of your location, Languid Touch activates: Causes that Survivor to suffer from the Exhausted Status Effect for 6 / 8 / 10 seconds. | Languid Touch has a cool-down of 5 seconds. "Submit to me. It is inevitable." — Vecna

Extends the duration of all instances of a Survivor Aura being revealed to you by +2 seconds. | Lethal Pursuer benefits from its own effect. "...it knows what it wants and won't stop till it gets it." — Carlos Oliveira

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 8.3.0 [ edit ] Rework: removed the Token system and changed the Perk to trigger its effect at full strength on whichever Survivor performs an Unhook action. Buff: now applies its effect at maximum strength at all times. This is equivalent to having accumulated 10 Tokens before the Rework.
Nerf: only applies to whichever Survivor performed the Unhook action instead of all Survivors.
Change: updated the flavour and lore texts.
Patch 9.2.0 [ edit ] Buff: doubled the Effect duration from 30 seconds to 60 seconds.
Nerf: reduced the Effect strength from 30 / 40 / 50 % to 20 / 25 / 30 %.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Lightborn
official_description: Unlike other beasts of The Fog, you have adapted to light. The Auras of Survivors attempting to blind you by any means are revealed to you for 6 / 8 / 10 seconds. | Grants immunity from being blinded from Flashlights, Firecrackers, Flash Grenades, and Blast Mine. | "These monsters... they adapt! They emerge with strange new abilities." — Vigo, Vigo's Journal

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.7.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: removed the dimmed vision debuff.
Buff: increased the Flashlight Blind recovery rate to 50 % across all Tiers.
Patch 2.6.2 [ edit ] Buff: increased the light resistance percentages to 60 / 70 / 80 %.
Patch 4.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: the Killer is now immune to getting blinded by Flashlights or Firecrackers.
Buff: added a secondary effect that reveals the Aura of Survivors attempting to blind the Killer.
Patch 5.0.0 [ edit ] Change: added Flashbangs and Blast Mines to the immunity effect.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Machine Learning
official_description: "Free from your shackles, you are born anew and prepared to eliminate your creators." After performing the Damage Generator action, Machine Learning activates: Causes that Generator to become a Compromised Generator. Damaging another Generator transfers the state to it instead. | | The Aura of the Compromised Generator is highlighted to you in yellow until it is completed. | Once the Compromised Generator is completed, you benefit from the following effects for 40 / 50 / 60 seconds: Undetectable Status Effect | +8 % Haste Status Effect | Machine Learning deactivates after use. "YOU WILL HOLD NO DOMINION OVER ME!" — HUX-A7-13

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 8.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: doubled the duration of the bonus effects from 20 / 25 / 30 seconds to 40 / 50 / 60 seconds.

Patch 8.3.0 [ edit ] Buff: now already compromises the Generator used to activate it, instead of the one after that. This change was motivated by the previous Activation condition making the Perk awkward to use.
Patch 8.3.2 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the duration of the bonus effects from 40 / 50 / 60 seconds to 35 / 40 / 45 seconds.
Patch 9.0.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Haste strength from +10 % to +8 %.
Reversal: reverted the duration of the bonus effects from 35 / 40 / 45 seconds back to 40 / 50 / 60 seconds.
<span class="mw-headline"

We're too good at it." — The Legion

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 3.1.0 [ edit ] Nerf: no longer causes the Killer's Movement Speed to change when performing a Basic Attack while carrying a Survivor. This change is considered a nerf as before this change, repeatedly missing Basic Attacks would cause the Killer to move at a faster pace than their regular Carrying speed would allow, due to the lack of cool-down.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.3.0 [ edit ] Buff: made the activation range 32 metres across all Tiers.
Quality of Life: now works like Hex: Devour Hope, in that the Perk Icon in the HUD now stays dark until the activation range is reached.
Unknown Patch [ edit ] Change: the cool-down is now variable across all Tiers.
Patch 4.7.0 [ edit ] Change: the afflicted Survivor will now scream audibly across the entire Map.

Patch 6.6.0 [ edit ] Change: modified the behaviour of the triggered Survivor screams to be consistent across all Unlockables: Survivor screams are now 3D with a maximum audible range of 19 metres (used to be a map-wide 2D scream).
Triggers a Loud Noise Notification.

Patch 6.6.2 [ edit ] Reversal: reverted the behaviour of Survivor screams to their original functionality. The reversal was motivated by the community's negative response to the range limit, which caused the Perks to end up weaker than before.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Merciless Storm
official_description: "Your horrible trauma is felt by everyone." Whenever a Generator reaches 90 % of Repair progression, Merciless Storm triggers its effect: All Survivors repairing that Generator are faced with a continuous stream of Skill Checks until the Generator is completed. | If any contributing Survivor fails a Skill Check or interrupts the action by any means, The Entity blocks the Generator for 16 / 18 / 20 seconds. | Merciless Storm can only trigger once per Generator per Trial.
description_ar: بيرك Merciless Storm يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Onryō. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.
summary: بيرك Merciless Storm يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Onryō. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.
usage_examples: استخدمه ضمن بيلد يضغط على المولدات أو يكشف أماكن الناجين.
راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.

name: Mindbreaker
official_description: "Your distressing presence drains and weakens your prey." While repairing a Generator, Survivors suffer from the following effects: Incurs the Blindness and Exhausted Status Effects. Both Status Effects linger for 3 / 4 / 5 seconds after ending the interaction. | | Mindbreaker cannot be used to override an existing Exhausted Status Effect, but pauses its timer instead. "The specimen's apparent telepathic ability can evoke feelings of dread and fatigue in nearby individuals." — Hawkins National Laboratory
description_ar: بيرك Mindbreaker يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Demogorgon. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 4.3.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Linger effect from 1 / 2 / 3 second(s) to 3 / 4 / 5 seconds.
Buff: removed the Progression threshold for the Perk to be active.

Patch 5.3.0 [ edit ] Retired: with the departure of CHAPTER 13: Stranger Things from Dead by Daylight, Mindbreaker was reworked into a General Killer Perk: Fearmonger. For Players who had owned CHAPTER 13: Stranger Things before its removal, Mindbreaker would still show the flavour text in the description, and the Character association with The Demogorgon.
Buff: now also applies the Blindness Status Effect.
Patch 7.3.3 [ edit ] Reinstation: reinstated Mindbreaker with the return of CHAPTER 13: Stranger Things. Renamed Fearmonger back to Mindbreaker.
Patch 7.4.0 [ edit ] Change: Mindbreaker is now once again a Unique Perk.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Monitor & Abuse
official_description: "Meticulous in your approach, terrifying in your application." While actively chasing a Survivor, Monitor & Abuse applies its primary effect: Increases your Terror Radius by 5 / 10 / 15 %. | While not actively chasing a Survivor, Monitor & Abuse applies its secondary effect: Reduces your Terror Radius by 15 / 20 / 25 %. | "It's time for your treatment!" — Herman Carter, The Doctor

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.6.0 [ edit ] Buff: changed the Terror-Radius modifiers to be a fixed value across all Tiers.
Quality of Life: updated the tier-based FOV modifiers to be numerical values.
Patch 7.5.0 [ edit ] Reversal: changed the Terror-Radius modifiers to be tier-based again, due to the removal of the FOV effect removing the formerly tier-based effect.
Nerf: removed the tier-based FOV modifiers. This change was motivated by the Developers implementing an FOV Slider for Killers, which is equal in strength to Shadowborn's old effect.

Patch 9.2.0 [ edit ] Rework: reworked the Perk to now modify the Terror Radius by percentages, rather than fixed values in metres, while keeping the general effect identical. However, this change also reworked the way the Perk works in code, removing the former discrepancy between the code and the in-game description (see Trivia section).
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Nemesis
official_description: "You seek retribution on those who have wronged you." Whenever a Survivor other than your Obsession blinds you by any means or stuns you with a Pallet or a Locker, Nemesis triggers its primary effect: Causes that Survivor to become your current Obsession. | Whenever your Obsession switches to a different Survivor by any means, Nemesis triggers its secondary effects: Causes that Survivor to suffer from the Oblivious Status Effect for 40 / 50 / 60 seconds. | Their Aura is revealed to you for 8 seconds. | You can only be obsessed with one Survivor at a time. "Only a fool would spit in a demon's face and declare victory." — Renjiro's Doctrine 4:9

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 8.6.0 [ edit ] Buff: doubled the Aura-reveal duration from 4 seconds from 8 seconds.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: No Holds Barred
official_description: "You induce mental suffering by crushing any hope of escape." Each time a Generator is completed, No Holds Barred calls upon The Entity for the following effects: Blocks the Generator with the most Progression for 15 / 20 / 25 seconds. | The Aura of the Blocked Generator is highlighted to you in white. |

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 8.0.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Effect's duration from 20 / 25 / 30 seconds to 15 / 20 / 25 seconds.

Patch 9.0.0 [ edit ] Change: following the departure of CHAPTER 21: Hellraiser™ from Dead by Daylight in March 2025, this Patch changes Deadlock depending on whether or not Players own the Chapter or not: For Players owning CHAPTER 21, the Perk remains unchanged as a Unique Perk belonging to The Cenobite. This includes the original name, icon and flavour text.
For all other Players, the Perk is changed into a General Perk available to all Killers, renaming it to No Holds Barred and giving it a new icon, replacing The Cenobite's face with a skull instead.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: No Quarter
official_description: "Every skirmish ended with a relentless volley of cannon fire, leaving no doubt who was superior." Whenever a Survivor healing themselves by any means reaches 75 % Healing Progress, No Quarter triggers its effect: The Survivor is faced with a continuous stream of Skill Checks until the Self-Heal is completed. | If they fail a Skill Check or interrupt the action by any means, they suffer from the Broken Status Effect for 20 / 25 / 30 seconds. | "Fire another broadside! Don't stop until the sea takes them!" — Portia Maye

name: No Way Out
official_description: "You are not going to let just anyone into the VIP room." Whenever you hook a Survivor for the first time, No Way Out gains +1 Token. Once the Exit Gates are powered, No Way Out activates: Causes the first Survivor to interact with either Exit Gate Switch to trigger a Loud Noise Notification at their location. | Upon triggering the Loud Noise Notification, No Way Out calls upon The Entity for the following effect: Blocks both Exit Gate Switches for 12 seconds. This time is extended by an additional 6 / 9 / 12 seconds per accumulated Token, up to a combined maximum of 36 / 48 / 60 seconds. | | "That sound when you bleed... let me hear it again." — Ji-Woon Hak

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 5.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the minimum Block duration from 10 seconds to 12 seconds.
Buff: increased the Token-base Bonus durations from 4 / 6 / 8 seconds to 6 / 9 / 12 seconds.
Buff: now postpones its activation until the first Survivor interacts with either Exit Gate Switch.
Buff: now triggers a Loud Noise Notification when the first Survivor interacts with either Exit Gate Switch.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: None Are Free
official_description: "You know better than anyone that the light at the end of the tunnel is only an illusion." Whenever you hook a Survivor for the first time, None Are Free gains +1 Token, up to a maximum of 4 Tokens. Once all Generators are completed, None Are Free calls upon The Entity to trigger the following effect: Blocks all Windows and upright Pallets for a stack-able 12 / 14 / 16 seconds per Token, up to a maximum of 48 / 56 / 64 seconds. | "I'm not wrong. What's wrong is this messed-up world!" — Ken Kaneki

official_description: "The machinations of the weak and craven draw your ire. Your anger forces Survivors to reveal themselves." name: Oppression
official_description: "Life has been difficult for you, so you will make it difficult for others." Performing the Damage Generator action on a Generator triggers the following effects: Randomly selects up to 4 additional Generators that will also start regressing. Triggers a difficult Skill Check for all Survivors currently repairing them. | | Oppression has a cool-down of 45 / 40 / 35 seconds. "Damn them as they would damn us." — Charlotte Deshayes

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 7.5.0 [ edit ] Change: only the kicked Generator will experience a Regression event, the four random Generators will not. This change is due to the addition of a limit to how often individual Generators can experience a Regression event, which would otherwise unfairly penalise the use of this Perk.
Patch 8.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: halved the Cool-down durations from 120 / 100 / 80 seconds to 60 / 50 / 40 seconds.
Patch 9.2.0 [ edit ] Buff: further reduced the Cool-down durations from 60 / 50 / 40 seconds to 45 / 40 / 35 seconds.
Buff: increased the number of potentially affected additional Generators from 3 to 4.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Overcharge
official_description: "You are fuelled by your hate for progress." Performing the Damage Generator action on a Generator applies Overcharge: The next Survivor interacting with that Generator is faced with a difficult Skill Check. Instantly regresses that Generator by 2 / 3 / 4 % of its maximum possible Progression in addition to the default Progression penalty. | | After Overcharge is applied to a Generator, the following effect also applies: Increases the Regression speed from 85 % to 130 % of its default value over the next 30 seconds. | "It is a trap, But a trap one must step in..." — Herman Carter
description_ar: بيرك Overcharge يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Doctor. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.
summary: بيرك Overcharge يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Doctor. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.
usage_examples: استخدمه ضمن بيلد يضغط على المولدات أو يكشف أماكن الناجين.
راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.6.3 [ edit ] Buff: changed the difficulty modifier to tremendously across all Tiers.
Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: added the effect of greatly increasing the Regression speed of the damaged Generator over a given period.
Patch 6.7.0 [ edit ] Buff: reduced the inital Regression speed penalty to 85%.
Nerf: reduced the maximum Regression speed bonus to 130%.
Nerf: reduced the penalty for missing the difficult Skill Check to 2 / 3 / 4 %.

Patch 7.5.0 [ edit ] Change: only the initiating kick counts as a Regression event, the potentially failed Skill Check will not. This change is due to the addition of a limit to how often individual Generators can experience a Regression event.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Overwhelming Presence
official_description: "Just knowing you are near is enough to disturb your victims." Whenever a Survivor starts using an Item within 32 metres of your location, Overwhelming Presence triggers its primary effect: Inflicts the Exhausted Status Effect for 15 seconds. | Whenever a Survivor within 32 metres of your location becomes Exhausted by any means, Overwhelming Presence triggers its secondary effect: The Aura of whichever Exhausted Survivor is closest to your location is revealed to you for 2 / 3 / 4 seconds. | Overwhelming Presence has a cool-down of 25 seconds. "Shit!! I dropped the gauze!"
description_ar: بيرك Overwhelming Presence يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Doctor. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.7.0 [ edit ] Buff: doubled the Effect strength from 40 / 45 / 50 % to 80 / 90 / 100 %.

Patch 3.4.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: no longer shows the debuff icon in the Survivors' HUD until the debuff is active. This change prevented Overwhelming Presence from acting as a visual warning whenever a Survivor entered the Terror Radius, even without using an Item.
Change: changed the introductory flavour text.

Patch 9.1.0 [ edit ] Rework: now causes Survivors using an Item while too close to the Killer to suffer from the Exhausted Status Effect and briefly reveal their Aura to them, instead of accelerating the Depletion rate of said Item while inside the Terror Radius.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Phantom Fear
official_description: "You have always had a reputation for startling unsuspecting guests." Whenever a Survivor within your Terror Radius looks at you, they suffer from the following effects: Causes them to scream and reveal their Aura to you for 2 seconds. | Phantom Fear has a cool-down of 80 / 70 / 60 seconds. "Excellent. Your screams please me." — Springtrap

name: Pop Goes the Weasel
official_description: "A deep bond with The Entity unlocks great strength." After hooking a Survivor, Pop Goes the Weasel activates for 35 / 40 / 45 seconds. While active, manually damaging a Generator triggers the following effect: Increases the regression amount by +15 % resulting in 20 % regression. | Pop Goes the Weasel deactivates after use or once its timer elapses.
description_ar: بيرك Pop Goes the Weasel يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Clown. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 3.0.0 [ edit ] Buff: modified the Progression penalty from a variable 15 / 20 / 25 % to a fixed -25 % across all Tiers.
Buff: modified the Activation timer from a fixed 30 seconds to a variable 40 / 50 / 60 seconds.
Patch 4.3.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Activation timer from 40 / 50 / 60 seconds to 35 / 40 / 45 seconds.
Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Progression penalty from -25 % to -20 %. This change compensates for the Damage Generator action now applying a small Progression penalty of -2.5 % by default.

Nerf: modified the Progression penalty to now be based on the Generator's current Progression, rather than its total possible Progression. This change intended to reduce the potency of the Perk on Generators with little Progression and to reward using the Perks on Generators close to completion.
Patch 7.0.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Progression penalty from -20 % to -30 %.
Patch 8.0.0 [ edit ] Reversal: reverted the Progression penalty back from -30 % to -20 %.
Patch 9.5.0 [ edit ] Rework - Buff: now increases the base Generator regression amount by +15 %, resulting in 20 % total regression.

name: Predator
official_description: "Your acute tracking ability allows you to find lost prey more easily." Whenever you lose a Survivor in a Chase, Predator triggers its effect: Their Aura is revealed to you for 4 seconds. | Predator has a cool-down of 60 / 50 / 40 seconds. "Never stop moving and hope you're always two steps ahead of the beast." — Unknown, Notebook
description_ar: بيرك Predator يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Wraith. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.
summary: بيرك Predator يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Wraith. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.1.0 [ edit ] Rework: now causes Scratch Marks to spawn slightly / moderately / considerably closer together than normal, making it easier for a Killer to track a Survivor's path.
Patch 8.3.0 [ edit ] Rework: now reveals the Aura of the Survivor who lost you in chase last, helping you potentially restart the chase, in exchange of the Perk going on a cool-down afterwards.
Patch 8.3.2 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Aura-reveal duration from 6 seconds to 4 seconds.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Rancor
official_description: Whenever a Generator is completed, Rancor triggers its primary effects: Causes all Survivors to scream and trigger a Loud Noise Notification at their current location. This effect lingers for 3 seconds. | | Your Aura is revealed to the Obsession for 5 / 4 / 3 seconds. | Once the Exit Gates are powered, Rancor additionally triggers its secondary effects on the Obsession: Inflicts the Exposed Status Effect for the remainder of the Trial. | Grants the ability to kill them when in close proximity. | You can only be obsessed with one Survivor at a time.
description_ar: بيرك Rancor يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Spirit. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 6.4.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: Expanded the Trigger condition of the secondary effects to include powering the Exit Gates by any means, rather than just completing the final Generator.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Rapid Brutality
official_description: You move with a speed and fury that makes blood run cold. You can no longer gain the Bloodlust Status Effect. | Successfully hitting a Survivor with a Basic Attack grants you a +5 % Haste Status Effect for 8 / 9 / 10 seconds. | "Catch it, put it in the airlock, get rid of it." — Dallas

name: Ravenous
official_description: "The scent of blood is in the air. Time to feast." Whenever you hook a Survivor for the first time, Ravenous gains +1 Token, up to a maximum of 4 Tokens. Once you accumulate 4 Tokens, all Survivors suffer from the following effects: Causes them to scream. | Inflicts the Exposed Status Effect for 40 / 50 / 60 seconds. | "Weeping Ginevra's blood streams/The devil smells the weak." — Fabbro Barone's "Ginevra"

name: Remember Me
official_description: Whenever the Obsession loses a Health State by any means, Remember Me gains +1 Token, up to a maximum of 3 / 4 / 5 Tokens: Increases the Opening time of both Exit Gates by a stack-able 6 seconds per Token, up to a maximum additional Opening time of 18 / 24 / 30 seconds. This results in a maximum possible Opening time of 38 / 44 / 50 seconds for both Exit Gates. | | Remember Me does not apply its penalty to the Obsession, allowing them to open both Exit Gates in the default time of 20 seconds. You can only be obsessed with one Survivor at a time. "You don't remember? You must. You're my number one, and you'll never wake up again." — Freddy Krueger
description_ar: بيرك Remember Me يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Nightmare. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.7.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the stack-able Penalty from +5 seconds to +4 seconds per Token.
Nerf: reduced the maximum Token counts from 4 / 5 / 6 Tokens to 2 / 3 / 4 Tokens.
Patch 5.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: now triggers on the Obsession losing a Health State by any means, rather than just on Basic Attacks.
Patch 7.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the stack-able Penalty from +4 seconds to +6 seconds per Token.
Patch 8.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the maximum Token counts from 2 / 3 / 4 Tokens to 3 / 4 / 5 Tokens.

Patch 9.0.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: updated the in-game description to more accurately reflect its effect. While the new in-game description does reflect the Perk's behaviour more accurately (i.e. colouring the Charge bar red to indicate a slower Action speed), its wording referring each Token slowing the Action by a stack-able percentage tends to confuse Players about the resulting duration, compared to the previous in-game description or the Wiki's current one, which both indicate a stack-able penalty in seconds.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Scourge Hook: Floods of Rage
official_description: "You form a psychic connection with The Entity and alter the rules of the Trial." At the start of the Trial, 4 Hooks in the environment are changed into Scourge Hooks: The Auras of these Scourge Hooks are highlighted in white. | Each time a Survivor is unhooked from a Scourge Hook, the following effects apply: The Auras of all other Survivors are revealed for 5 / 6 / 7 seconds. |
description_ar: بيرك Scourge Hook: Floods of Rage يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Onryō. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

name: Scourge Hook: Hangman's Trick
official_description: "Your ingenious modifications to hooks alert you of tampering." At the start of the Trial, 4 Hooks in the environment are changed into Scourge Hooks: The Auras of these Scourge Hooks are highlighted in white. | While carrying a Survivor, Scourge Hook: Hangman's Trick triggers its primary effect: The Auras of all Survivors within 12 / 14 / 16 metres of a Scourge Hook are revealed to you. | Whenever a Survivor starts the Sabotage action on any Hook, Scourge Hook: Hangman's Trick triggers its secondary effect: Triggers a Loud Noise Notification at the location of that Hook. | "No excuses, no equivocations... No crying." — Amanda Young
description_ar: بيرك Scourge Hook: Hangman's Trick يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Pig. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.
summary: بيرك Scourge Hook: Hangman's Trick يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Pig. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.1.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life (obsolete): now also caused sabotaged Bear Traps to auto-repair, but on a fixed timer of 60 seconds.
Patch 3.6.0 [ edit ] Rework: no longer reduces the Auto-Repair timer; now reveals the Auras of Survivors who are in proximity to Hooks while the Killer is carrying someone.
Patch 7.1.0 [ edit ] Rework: changed Hangman's Trick into a Scourge Hook Perk: Buff: increased the Aura-reveal ranges from 2 / 4 / 6 metres to 8 / 10 / 12 metres.
Nerf: now only applies the Aura-reveal effect to Scourge Hooks.
Patch 9.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: further increased the Aura-reveal ranges from 8 / 10 / 12 metres to 12 / 14 / 16 metres.

name: Scourge Hook: Monstrous Shrine
official_description: "Your fervent care of the Hooks found in the Basement has aroused The Entity's interest." At the start of the Trial, the 4 Hooks located inside the Basement and an additional 4 Hooks located throughout the Trial Grounds are all changed into Scourge Hooks: The Auras of Scourge Hooks are highlighted to you in white. | Whenever you are farther than 24 metres from a Survivor hooked onto a Scourge Hook, the following effect applies: Accelerates their Sacrifice Process by 10 / 15 / 20 %. | "Then you will know that there is no escape. When hanging in the depths, you face the dark one."

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Rework: changed Monstrous Shrine into Scourge Hook: Monstrous Shrine. Buff: now also converts 4 random Hooks in the environment in addition to the Basement Hooks.
Buff: increased the Sacrifice-Process acceleration from 3 / 6 / 9 % to 10 / 15 / 20 %.
Nerf: removed the secondary and tertiary effects of making Self-Unhooks more difficult to achieve and more punishing to attempt.
Nerf: added a distance-stipulation to the primary effect.

Calculations [ edit ] Hook Stages are analogous to a reservoir of 70 Charges, with each Survivor starting the Trial with 2 of them, for a total amount of 140 Charges. Whenever a Survivor is hooked, the Hook drains those Charges at a rate of -1 c/s (i.e. the Sacrifice Process). Scourge Hook: Monstrous Shrine accelerates the Sacrifice Process by increasing that Depletion rate to -1.1 / -1.15 / -1.2 c/s: Tier Depletion Rate Duration per Hook State Duration of the Sacrifice Process Default -1 c/s 70 seconds 140 seconds Tier I -1.1 c/s ~63.64 seconds ~127.27 seconds Tier II -1.15 c/s ~60.87 seconds ~121.74 seconds Tier III -1.2 c/s ~58.33 seconds 116.67 seconds <span class="mw-headline"

name: Scourge Hook: Pain Resonance
official_description: Overwhelming pain reverberates outwards. At the start of the Trial, 4 Hooks in the environment are changed into Scourge Hooks: The Auras of these Scourge Hooks are highlighted in white. | Start the Trial with 4 Token. Whenever a Survivor is hooked onto a Scourge Hook for the first time, Scourge Hook: Pain Resonance consumes -1 Token and applies the following effects: Causes the Generator with the most progression to explode, damaging it. Instantly regresses the Damaged Generator by 10 / 15 / 20 % of its total Progression. | Causes the Damaged Generator to start regressing afterwards. | | Causes all Survivors repairing the Damaged Generator to scream. This does not trigger a Loud Noise Notification. | | Scourge Hook: Pain Resonance is disabled for the remainder of the Trial once all Tokens are consumed. "The pain spills beyond flesh/darkness staining the horizon." — Girl in the Shadow by Carmina Mora

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Nerf: no longer causes the targeted Generator to explode, causing repairing Survivors to no longer be interrupted, scream, and reveal their location to the Killer. This nerf was motivated by the Developers no longer wishing for the Perk to reveal the repairing Survivors' locations, giving the Killer intel on whether the Generator was being worked on or not.

Patch 6.1.2 [ edit ] Partial Reversal: partially reverted the previous nerf, once again causing the Perk to explode the targeted Generator, which in turn once again interrupts the repairing Survivors and causes them to scream, but suppresses the Loud Noise Notification that usually accompanies the latter. This change allowed the Developers to keep the main intention of the previous nerf while removing an unintended side-effect of not interrupting Survivors. The latter had allowed for some unfortunate situations in which the presence of Merciless Storm could cause repairing Survivors to face up to 40 of its Skill Checks.

Patch 6.7.0 [ edit ] Rework: reworked the Perk's Activation condition to be based on Tokens, to reduce the number of possible activations, but greatly increased the Regression penalty in exchange. Buff: increased the Regression penalty from 9 / 12 / 15 % to 15 / 20 / 25 %.
Nerf: now only triggers when a Survivor is hooked onto a Scourge Hook for the first time, which consumes a Token and caps it at one activation per Survivor at the most.

Patch 7.5.0 [ edit ] Change: no longer triggers a Loud Noise Notification on the exploding Generator itself, if no actual Progression is lost (i.e. when it is blocked or has reached the limit of possible Regression Events ).
Patch 8.0.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Regression penalty from 15 / 20 / 25 % to 10 / 15 / 20 %.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Action Speed penalty from 7 / 8 / 9 % to 10 / 13 / 16 %.

Patch 7.6.0 [ edit ] Nerf: the Status Effects are now timer-based, instead of lasting until the Survivor is healed. This change was motivated by a general change in design-philosophy for the Mangled Status Effect.

Patch 9.0.0 [ edit ] Change: following the departure of CHAPTER 21: Hellraiser™ from Dead by Daylight in March 2025, this Patch changes Scourge Hook: Gift of Pain depending on whether or not Players own the Chapter or not: For Players owning CHAPTER 21, the Perk remains unchanged as a Unique Perk belonging to The Cenobite. This includes the original name, icon and flavour text.

For all other Players, the Perk is changed into a General Perk available to all Killers, renaming it to Scourge Hook: Weeping Wounds and giving it a new icon, replacing The Cenobite's chains with Entity-claws instead.

name: See How They Run
official_description: Losing your Obsession in a chase causes See How They Run to gain +1 Token, up to a maximum of 3 Tokens, and performing a Basic or Special Attack with the potential to damage a Survivor causes it to lose -1 Token. While having at least 1 Token, you benefit from the following effect: Grants a stack-able 3 / 4 / 5 % Haste Status Effect per Token, up to a maximum of 9 / 12 / 15 %. | See How They Run has a cool-down of 10 seconds between gaining additional Tokens. You can only be obsessed with one Survivor at a time.
description_ar: بيرك See How They Run يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح.  أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Hotfix 1.2.1d [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the stack-able Movement Speed bonus from 5 / 6 / 7 % to 3 / 4 / 5 %.
Nerf: added a short cool-down between the Perk being able to gain additional Tokens.
Unknown Patch [ edit ] Change: updated the Perk with the Haste Status Effect.

Patch 3.7.0 [ edit ] Change: updated the description to specify Basic or Special Attacks, rather than just referring to "offensive actions". Contrary to the in-game description, not all Special Attacks cause See How They Run to lose a Token, just those that have the potential to actually damage a Survivor.

Patch 9.4.0 [ edit ] Change: following the departure of CHAPTER 2: The HALLOWEEN® Chapter from Dead by Daylight in January 2026, this Patch changes Play With Your Food depending on whether or not Players own the Chapter or not: For Players owning CHAPTER 2, the Perk remains unchanged as a Unique Perk belonging to The Shape. This includes the original name and flavour text.
For all other Players, the Perk is changed into a General Perk available to all Killers, renaming it to See How They Run.

name: Septic Touch
official_description: "The Land of the Shadows subsumes all. Healing only delays the inevitable." Whenever a Survivor performs a Healing action while inside the Terror Radius, they suffer from the following effects: Inflicts the Blindness and Exhausted Status Effects. راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.0.5 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the bonus FOV values from 10 / 20 / 30 ° to 3 / 5 / 10 °.

Patch 1.5.0 [ edit ] Change: updated several aspects of Shadowborn to account for general changes to the lighting engine in the same Patch. Buff: reduced the Blinding Time Reduction penalty from -50 % to -30 %.
Buff: reduced the Blinded Recovery Time penalty from 25 / 50 / 75 % to 30 / 40 / 50 %.
Nerf: no longer increases the visual brightness of the environment.
Patch 1.8.3 [ edit ] Buff: removed the Blinding Time Reduction penalty.
Buff: removed the Blinded Recovery Time penalty.
Buff: increased bonus FOV values from 3 / 5 / 10 ° to 9 / 12 / 15 °.
Patch 2.6.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: changed the FOV modifiers from adjectives to numerical values.

name: Shattered Hope
official_description: To capture your prey, you must first extinguish their hope. Instead of snuffing Boon Totems you destroy them. | Destroying a Boon Totem this way reveals the Auras of all Survivors that were within its range at that moment for 6 / 7 / 8 seconds. | "Nothing is permanent here. Nothing except our endless perdition." — Unknown, Notebook

name: Sloppy Butcher
official_description: "You know where to hit to make them bleed." Survivors hit with your vicious Basic Attack suffer from the following effects for 70 / 80 / 90 seconds: Inflicts the Haemorrhage and Mangled Status Effects. | Modifies the potency of Haemorrhage: Increases the Spawn frequency of Pools of Blood by 50 / 75 / 100 %. | Increases the Regression rate of partial Healing progression by +25 %. | | "It is in its sadistic nature. There is no swift kill as it delights in the obscene spectacle of our agonising suffering."

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.0.5 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Spawn frequency of Pools of Blood for Tier I and Tier II (unknown values).
Nerf: reduced the Spawn frequency of Pools of Blood for Tier III (unknown value).
Patch 1.5.2 [ edit ] Change: updated the Perk with the Haemorrhage Status Effect.
Patch 2.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: now also applies the Mangled Status Effect.

Patch 4.5.2 [ edit ] Quality of Life: updated how the Perk affected Pools of Blood to make it more consistent in use. Previously, it would change values for the minimum and maximum delays between successive blood-puddles spawning, whereas it now affects the spawn frequency in general.

Patch 5.7.0 [ edit ] Buff: added a new effect that affects that increases the potency of Haemorrhage. This change was motivated by a rework to the Haemorrhage Status Effect, which causes it to regress any partial Healing progress for affected Survivors.
Patch 7.6.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased and modified the boost to Haemorrhage from a variable 15 / 20 / 25 % to a fixed +25 % across all Tiers.

Indirect Nerf: all effects are now based on the timer of the two Status Effects, rather than lasting until the Survivor is fully healed. This nerf was motivated by a rework to the Mangled Status Effect (and Haemorrhage by association) in the same Patch that made it timer-based.

name: Spies from the Shadows
official_description: "Allows the Crows found in the environment to directly communicate with you." Whenever a Survivor startles a Crow within 20 / 28 / 36 metres of your location, Spies from the Shadows triggers its effect: Alerts you with a unique Loud Noise Notification in the shape of a Crow. | Spies from the Shadows has a cool-down of 5 seconds between alerts. "In the shadows they torment, scarring our minds with each scream."
description_ar: بيرك Spies from the Shadows يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح.  أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.0.2 [ edit ] Quality of Life: no longer triggers on Crows startled by the Killer.
Patch 1.0.5 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Trigger chance on Tier I from 25 % to 50 %.
Buff: increased the Trigger range on Tier I from 12 metres to 16 metres.
Patch 2.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased and modified the Trigger chance from a variable 50 / 50 / 75 % to a fixed 100 % across all Tiers.
Buff: increased the Trigger range from 16 / 24 / 36 metres to 32 / 40 / 48 metres.
Quality of Life: made the icon of the Loud Noise Notification a Crow to visually distinguish it from other Loud Noise Notifications.

Patch 2.6.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: added a short cool-down of 5 seconds in-between Loud Noise Notifications to stop multiple startled Crows from spamming the notification and overwhelming the Player.
Nerf: reduced the Trigger range from 32 / 40 / 48 metres to 20 / 28 / 36 metres.

name: Spirit Fury
official_description: "Each obstacle you break magnifies the wrath of The Entity." After manually breaking a total of 4 / 3 / 2 dropped Pallets by any means, Spirit Fury activates: Instantly breaks the next Pallet used by a Survivor to stun you. | Spirit Fury deactivates after use and does not affect the duration of the Pallet Stun. "Fury is her blood."

name: Starstruck
official_description: "Your unmatched showmanship dazzles all." Whenever you are carrying a Survivor, Starstruck activates: Causes all other Survivors in your Terror Radius to suffer from the Exposed Status Effect. This effect lingers for 26 / 28 / 30 seconds after affected Survivors leave the Terror Radius or Starstruck deactivates. | | Starstruck deactivates after hooking or dropping the carried Survivor and has a cool-down of 60 seconds. "Enjoy this moment, few get so close." — Ji-Woon Hak

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 4.6.0 [ edit ] Buff: reduced the PTB-duration of the cool-down from 90 seconds to 60 seconds for release.
Buff: the Exposed Status Effect now refreshes when Survivors re-enter the Terror Radius, as opposed to only applying once on the PTB.

name: Stridor
official_description: You are acutely sensitive to the breathing of your prey. Increases the volume of Grunts of Pain of injured Survivors by 30 / 40 / 50 %. | Increases the volume of regular breathing of Survivors by 15 / 20 / 25 %. | "If you do not stop and catch your breath... she will."

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 5.1.0 [ edit ] Nerf: changed Stridor to apply its effect as a multiplier, rather than them being additive. This meant that Stridor could no longer counter effects that reduced a Survivor's Grunts of Pain to 0 % (e.g. Iron Will ).
Patch 8.1.1 [ edit ] Buff: adjusted the values to close the gaps between Tiers to make lower tiered versions of Stridor more useful. Increased Grunts of Pain from 25 / 50 / 50 % to 30 / 40 / 50 %.
Increased regular breathing from 0 / 0 / 25 % to 15 / 20 / 25 %.

Reversal: reverted the previous nerf from three years earlier, making Stridor add its effect instead of multiplying it with others. This means that if another Perk reduces the volume to 0 %, Stridor can raise it to reduced levels of up to 50 % again.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Superior Anatomy
official_description: "Decades of research have culminated in you: something faster, stronger, and more dangerous than any human." Whenever a Survivor performs a Rush Vault action within 12 metres of your location, Superior Anatomy activates: Increases your Vaulting speed by 30 / 35 / 40 %. | Superior Anatomy deactivates after vaulting. Superior Anatomy has a cool-down of 25 seconds.

Vaulting Speeds [ edit ] Vaulting Speed Default Vaulting Time 1.700s Superior Anatomy I (-30 %) 1.190s Superior Anatomy II (-35 %) 1.105s Superior Anatomy III (-40 %) 1.020s <span class="mw-headline"

name: Surge
official_description: "Your eerie presence charges the air and interferes with technology." Whenever you put a Survivor into the Dying State with your Basic Attack, Surge triggers its effect: Causes all Generators within 32 metres of your location to instantly explode and start regressing. | Instantly regresses all affected Generators by 6 / 7 / 8 %. | "The creature appears to have some unknown influence over electricity and nearby electronics." — Hawkins National Laboratory
description_ar: بيرك Surge يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Demogorgon. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 5.3.0 [ edit ] Retired: with the departure of CHAPTER 13: Stranger Things from Dead by Daylight, Surge was reworked into a General Killer Perk: Jolt. For Players who had owned CHAPTER 13: Stranger Things before its removal, Jolt would still show the original flavour text in the description and the Character association with The Demogorgon.
Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: removed the cool-down from the Perk.
Nerf: instead of a fixed penalty of -8 % across all Tiers, Surge (then Jolt) now applies a tiered penalty of 6 / 7 / 8 %.
Patch 7.0.0 [ edit ] Undocumented Buff: Survivors now scream in addition to being interrupted by the exploding Generator.
Patch 7.3.3 [ edit ] Reinstation: reinstated Surge with the return of CHAPTER 13: Stranger Things. Renamed Jolt back to Surge.
Patch 7.4.0 [ edit ] Change: is now once again a Unique Perk.

Patch 7.5.0 [ edit ] Change: does not trigger a Loud Noise Notification if no Progression is lost on a Generator. This change is due to the addition of a limit to how often individual Generators can experience a Regression event.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Surveillance
official_description: Unlocks potential in your Aura-reading ability: The Auras of damaged Generators are highlighted based on their current state: White Aura: The Generator is currently regressing. | Yellow Aura: The Generator stopped regressing due to Survivor-intervention. This effect lasts for 8 / 12 / 16 seconds, after which the Generator Aura returns to its default colour. | | While Survivors are repairing Generators, they suffer from the following effect: Increases the audible range of all noises related to the Repair interaction by +8 metres. | "Are you gonna behave?" — Amanda Young
description_ar: بيرك Surveillance يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Pig. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.5.0 [ edit ] Rework: made the Perk more useful by making its primary effect last longer and adding secondary effects that provide additional information to the Killer and penalties to the Survivors. Buff: now permanently highlights the Auras of all regressing Generators in the environment in white.
Buff: now highlights the Auras of Generators that were stopped from regressing by Survivors in yellow.
Buff: now increases the audible range of Repair noises.

Patch 4.3.0 [ edit ] Indirect Nerf: Generators regressing back to 0 %, which causes them to stop regressing without Survivor-intervention, now also trigger the secondary effect. This change was an unfortunate side-effect of the standardisation to both Generator Terminology and to the underlying mechanics of effects that affect Generators in the same Patch.
Patch 5.0.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: Generators stopping to regress at 0 % no longer trigger the secondary effect, making Survivor-intervention its exclusive trigger.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Terminus
official_description: "You are a brilliant tactician who prepares for every eventuality." Once the Exit Gates are powered, Terminus activates: Causes all injured, dying, or hooked Survivors to suffer from the Broken Status Effect. This effect last until one of the Exit Gates is opened, after which it lingers for another 35 / 40 / 45 seconds. | |
description_ar: بيرك Terminus يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Mastermind. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 9.0.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Broken duration from 20 / 25 / 30 seconds to 35 / 40 / 45 seconds.
Patch 9.5.0 [ edit ] Buff: Broken effect is now triggered earlier, preventing healing effects ( Adrenaline ) from triggering simultaneously.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Territorial Imperative
official_description: Unlocks potential in your Aura-reading ability. Whenever a Survivor enters the Basement while you are farther than 24 metres from its entrance, Territorial Imperative activates: The Aura of that Survivor is revealed to you for 4 / 5 / 6 seconds. | Territorial Imperative has a cool-down of 45 seconds. "We're not safe anywhere..."

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 3.0.0 [ edit ] Buff: lowered the cool-downs to 30 / 25 / 20 seconds.
Patch 7.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the duration of the Aura reveal to 4 / 5 / 6 seconds.
Buff: reduced the distance the Killer must be from the basement entrance to 24 metres.
Nerf: increased the cool-down to 45 seconds.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Thanatophobia
official_description: "Their courage fades in the face of their undeniable mortality." Whenever at least one Survivor is either injured, dying, or hooked, Thanatophobia activates. While active, all Survivors suffer from the following primary effect: Incurs a stack-able 1 / 1.5 / 2 % Action-Speed penalty per injured, dying, or hooked Survivor for Cleansing, Repairing, and Sabotaging, up to a maximum of 4 / 6 / 8 %. | If all four Survivors are either injured, dying, or hooked, they suffer from the following secondary effect: Increases the penalty by an additional +12 % to a total of 16 / 18 / 20 %. | "She plays with us and revels in our pain."

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 2.6.0 [ edit ] Rework: reworked the penalty modifiers to be based on stacks of 3 / 3.5 / 4 % per injured, dying, or hooked Survivor, up to a maximum of 12 / 14 / 16 %.
Patch 4.3.0 [ edit ] Buff: now affects the Cleansing speed of Totems.
Buff: increased the Penalty percentages from 3 / 3.5 / 4 % to 4 / 4.5 / 5 %.
Nerf: no longer affects Healing speeds.
Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: further increased the Penalty percentages from 4 / 4.5 / 5 % to 4.5 / 5 / 5.5 %.

Patch 6.1.2 [ edit ] Buff: added a secondary effect that increased the final penalty by an additional -12 % if all four Survivors are either injured, dying, or hooked. This buff compensates for the nerf below.
Nerf: drastically reduced the strength of the primary effect from 4 / 4.5 / 5 % to 1 / 1.5 / 2 %.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Thrilling Tremors
official_description: "Your dark designs and shrewd composure rouse The Entity." After picking up a Survivor, Thrilling Tremors triggers its effects: Blocks all Generators not currently being repaired by any Survivors for 16 seconds. | The Auras of Blocked Generators are highlighted to you in white. | Thrilling Tremors has a cool-down of 40 / 35 / 30 seconds. "The night assists me and it's endless here." — The Ghost Face

<span class="mw-headline"

name: THWACK!
official_description: "Your prey is terrified by your relentless pursuit." Start the Trial with 3 Tokens. Whenever you break a Breakable Wall or a dropped Pallet, THWACK! consumes -1 Token and applies the following effect: Causes all Survivors within 36 metres of your location to scream and reveal their Aura for 4 / 5 / 6 seconds. | Whenever you hook a Survivor, THWACK! is granted +1 Token. "All those who stood in her path feared her mighty THWACK!" — Sonhadores Sombrios, Issue #3
description_ar: بيرك THWACK! يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Skull Merchant. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 8.3.0 [ edit ] Rework: now uses the Token system. Buff: allows the Perk User to store Tokens and use the Perk potentially more than once per chase and when it is more likely that they will get some use out of it.
Buff: modified and increased the Aura-reveal duration from a fixed 4 seconds across all Tiers to a variable 3 / 4 / 5 seconds.
Nerf: modified and reduced the Activation range from a variable 28 / 30 / 32 metres to a fixed 24 metres across all Tiers.
Patch 9.0.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Activation range from 24 metres to 36 metres.
Buff: further increased the Aura-reveal duration from 3 / 4 / 5 seconds to 4 / 5 / 6 seconds.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Tinkerer
official_description: Whenever a Generator is repaired to 70 %, you benefit from the following effects: Triggers a Loud Noise Notification for that Generator, revealing its location. | Grants the Undetectable Status Effect for the next 12 / 14 / 16 seconds. | Tinkerer can only trigger once per Generator per Trial. "The Hillbilly makes impressive tools out of scraps. Tools aimed at maiming us in creative ways." — Notebook
description_ar: بيرك Tinkerer يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Hillbilly. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.3.0 [ edit ] Quality of Life: Tinkerer now only affects modifiers positively.
Nerf: decreased the bonus applied to modifiers to 6 / 8 / 10 %.
Buff: Tinkerer' s bonus is now additive.
Patch 2.1.0 [ edit ] Rework: Tinkerer now gives the Killer a noise notification if a Generator is repaired to 85 % and also reduces their Terror Radius to 0 metres for 8 / 10 / 12 seconds.
Patch 3.4.0 [ edit ] Change: updated Tinkerer with the Undetectable Status Effect.
Patch 4.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: lowered the activation threshold to 70 %.
Buff: increased the Undetectable duration.

Patch 6.1.0 [ edit ] Nerf: Tinkerer can now only be triggered once per Generator, instead of triggering each time the Generator is progressed back to the activation threshold after having regressed below it.

name: Trail of Torment
official_description: "You guide your victims along a path of pain and punishment." Performing the Damage Generator action on a Generator activates Trail of Torment: Grants the Undetectable Status Effect. | The Aura of the Damaged Generator is revealed to all Survivors and highlighted in yellow. | Trail of Torment deactivates after the Damaged Generator stops regressing by any means and has a cool-down of 60 / 45 / 30 seconds between activations.

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 4.3.0 [ edit ] Buff: now applies the Undetectable Status Effect for the entire duration the Generator is regressing.
Nerf: now deactivates whenever the Generator is being repaired or a Survivor is hit by any means.
Patch 7.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: no longer deactivates whenever a Survivor is hit by any means.
Patch 8.1.0 [ edit ] Buff: reduced the Cool-down duration from 80 / 70 / 60 seconds to 60 / 45 / 30 seconds.

name: Ultimate Weapon
official_description: "When they spot you, they know the end is near." Whenever you search a Locker, Ultimate Weapon triggers its effects on all Survivors within 40 metres of its location: Causes them to scream and reveal their location to you. | Inflicts the Blindness Status Effect for 30 seconds. | Ultimate Weapon has a cool-down of 55 / 50 / 45 seconds. "I can't lie to you about your chances, but... you have my sympathies." — Ash

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 7.7.0 [ edit ] Nerf: doubled the Cool-down durations from 40 / 35 / 30 seconds to 80 / 70 / 60 seconds.
Nerf: instead of causing Survivors to scream and reveal their location, while interrupting their interactions, they now have their Aura revealed instead.
Nerf: halved the activation time after opening a Locker from 30 seconds to 15 seconds.
Patch 7.7.1 [ edit ] Nerf: removed the Linger effect for the Perk's main effect.
Reversal: again causes Survivors entering the Activation radius to scream and reveal their location, instead of revealing their Aura.

Change: changed the Activation radius from being tied to the Terror Radius of the Killer to now be fixed to the Locker's location. This change retains the previous change's intended effect of nerfing the Perk for high-mobility Killers, while also buffing it for Killers with small Terror Radii or who lack mobility.
Patch 9.2.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Activation radius from 32 metres, to 40 metres.
Buff: reduced the Cool-down durations from 80 / 70 / 60 seconds to 55 / 50 / 45 seconds.

name: Unbound
official_description: "Pain lingers in the air, an alluring aroma that excites the impulses." When a Survivor becomes injured by any means, Unbound activates for 24 / 27 / 30 seconds: Vaulting a Window while Unbound is active grants a +7 % Haste Status Effect for 10 seconds. This effect cannot stack with itself. | | "I heard it. Running. It was getting closer!" — Anonymous Testimony, 09/09/1979

Patch 9.0.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Haste strength from +10 % to +7 %.

name: Undone
official_description: "Hope is the delusion that keeps its victims alive." For every Repair or Healing Skill Check failed by a Survivor, Undone gains +3 Tokens, up to a maximum of 18 / 24 / 30 Tokens. Performing the Damage Generator action on a Generator consumes all accumulated Tokens and applies the following effects: Instantly regresses the Damaged Generator by a stack-able -1 % of its total Progression per Token, up to a maximum of 18 / 24 / 30 %. | Blocks the Damaged Generator for 1 second per Token, up to a maximum of 18 / 24 / 30 seconds. | Causes the Damaged Generator to start regressing once it becomes unblocked. | Undone has a cool-down of 60 seconds. "Erratic behavior in machinery could be related to [REDACTED], further testing required." — OSS Report, 12/02/1943
description_ar: بيرك Undone يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح. يرتبط هذا البيرك بشخصية The Unknown. أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

name: Unforeseen
official_description: "Terror takes many forms and whispers devastating lies in its victims' ears." After performing the Damage Generator action on a Generator, you benefit from the following effects for 22 / 26 / 30 seconds: Transfers your Terror Radius to the damaged Generator. This Terror Radius is set to 32 metres. | | Grants the Undetectable Status Effect. | Unforeseen has a cool-down of 30 seconds. "Mom, if you find this, I'm sorry..." — Final Journal Entry, 07/12/1991

name: Unnerving Presence
official_description: "Your presence alone instils great fear." While inside your Terror Radius, Survivors performing Repairing or Healing actions suffer from the following effects: Increases the chances of triggering a Skill Check by +10 %. | Reduces the Success zone of those Skill Checks by 40 / 50 / 60 %. | "Its presence befalls us."

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.0.5 [ edit ] Buff: Activation chance reworked from being a multiplier to being a static increase to the base Activation chance. Net result is that the Activation chance is significantly higher than it used to be.
Patch 1.7.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Trigger Chance increase from 3 / 5 / 8 % to 6 / 8 / 10 %.
Buff: increased the Success Zone reduction to from 5 / 10 / 15 % to 40 / 50 / 60 %.
Patch 2.6.2 [ edit ] Buff: changed the Trigger Chance increase from a variable 6 / 8 / 10 % to a fixed +10 % across all Tiers.

Patch 3.4.0 [ edit ] Change: changed the Perk's interaction with the Perk Effect System, now only revealing its icon to affected Survivors when they are actually performing any of the actions listed in the Perk's description. Indirect Buff: this change addressed a side-effect of the old interaction acting as a visual warning of a Survivor having entered the Terror Radius.
Indirect Nerf: this change also caused Unnerving Presence to no longer affect Skill Checks that only feature a Great Success Zone.
Patch 3.6.0 [ edit ] Indirect Change: no longer lists the Sabotage action as it no longer procs any Skill Checks.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Unrelenting
official_description: "You recuperate faster from missed attacks made with your main weapon." Basic Attacks benefit from the following effect: Reduces the Cool-down time of missed attacks by 20 / 25 / 30 %. |
description_ar: بيرك Unrelenting يعطي الكيلر أداة لعب مرتبطة بتأثيره الأساسي. اقرأ الوصف الرسمي والقيم الرقمية قبل إدخاله في البيلد، ثم جرّبه مع بيركات تكمل نفس الهدف: معلومات، مطاردة، إبطاء، إنقاذ، أو إصلاح.  أفضل استخدام له يكون عندما تبني أسلوب لعبك حول الشرط المذكور في الوصف بدل اعتباره تأثيرًا عشوائيًا.

راقب شرط تفعيل البيرك ولا تهدره في لحظة لا تعطي قيمة.
patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 1.2.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Cool-down Time reduction for missed attacks from 3 / 6 / 9 % to 16 / 22 / 30 %.
Buff: increased the Cool-down Time reduction for successful attacks from 2 / 4 / 6 % to 10 / 16 / 22 %.
Patch 1.4.0 [ edit ] Buff: further increased the Cool-down Time reduction for missed attacks from 16 / 22 / 30 % to 20 / 25 / 30 %.

Nerf: no longer affects successful attacks. This nerf was motivated by the excessive use of the so-called "Machine Gun Combo" amongst Killer Players since the introduction of Save the Best for Last. Since both Perks had complementary effects, stacking them together reduced the Cool-down Time of Basic Attacks to problematic levels.
<span class="mw-headline"

name: Wandering Eye
official_description: "Bloodied prey surrounds you, vying for your attention." Whenever you start chasing a Survivor, Wandering Eye triggers the following effect: The Auras of all other injured Survivors within 20 metres of your location are revealed to you for 5 seconds. | Wandering Eye has a cool-down of 40 / 35 / 30 seconds. "A drop does not satiate/when a river is needed." — Elliot Campbell's "Grandeur of Samuel"

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 9.2.0 [ edit ] Buff: increased the Aura-reveal range from its PTB-value of 16 metres to 20 metres for release.

name: Weave Attunement
official_description: "You are so attuned to magical energies that you can sense even minor ripples around enchantments." Whenever an Item is depleted for the first time, it is automatically dropped: The Auras of dropped Survivor Items are revealed to you, as well as the Auras of any Survivors within 12 metres of them. Affected Survivors see the Aura of that Item as well. | | Causes Survivors picking up a Survivor Item to suffer from the Oblivious Status Effect for 20 / 25 / 30 seconds. | "No number of novelties will protect you from me!" — Vecna

patch_history: Change Log [ edit ] Patch 8.4.0 [ edit ] Nerf: reduced the Survivor Aura-reveal distance around dropped Items from 12 metres to 8 metres.
Nerf: Survivors now see the Aura of the Item that is revealing them.
Patch 8.4.1 [ edit ] Reversal: reverted the Survivor Aura-reveal distance around dropped Items from 8 metres back to 12 metres.

name: Whispers
official_description: "You have a rudimentary understanding of The Entity's voice." Whenever any Survivor is within 48 / 40 / 32 metres of your location, Whispers triggers its effect: Causes you to hear the sporadic whispers of The Entity, indicating a Survivor's presence. | "It is unclear as to the motivations of The Fog, but it is undeniable that it often takes the beast's side." — Unknown, Notebook

name: Zanshin Tactics
official_description: "You are mentally alert and aware of key points on the battlefield and nearby enemies in retreat." Unlocks potential in your Aura-reading ability: The Auras of Pallets and Windows are revealed to you within 32 metres. | Whenever a Survivor drops a Pallet, Zanshin Tactics triggers the following effect: Their Aura is revealed to you for 3 / 4 / 5 seconds. | "Within your enemy's strength is weakness." — Renjiro's Doctrine 12:5
